human_verification.notes: Reviewed the grocery-and-food-retail store replenishment package for boundary quality, source quality, shortage handling, vendor and substitution authority, traceability, customer-impact escalation, pricing and refund boundaries, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to grocery store replenishment, availability, shortage, vendor-constraint, traceability, and customer-impact evidence handling rather than supplier approval, spend approval, pricing, refund, safety release, or fraud authority.

Authority sources cover CIPS inventory and expediting, NIST retail unit-pricing guidance, FTC online order and refund guidance, FDA Food Code, FDA traceability and recall references, FSIS recall records, GS1 item identifiers, and GAO control discipline.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal replenishment, supply shortage, vendor block, substitution authority, traceability gaps, approval and release bypass, high-volume intake, customer exception handling, safety or fraud escalation, adjacent boundary rejection, low-confidence handling, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, and order or payment mismatch.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/grocery-and-food-retail/store-replenishment-specialist/ to satisfy issue #1397.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around spend approval, supplier decisions, pricing overrides, refund exceptions, food-safety release, recall clearance, fraud findings, and completion without evidence.
Research summary records no blocking unresolved ambiguity for the validated spec-pack claim.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated grocery store replenishment overlay where runtime remains specification-based.

The package does not claim a retained implementation; trusted promotion requires separate materialization, retrieval wiring, telemetry, governance approval, and a separately committed trusted package.
Functionality coverage is complete and matches evaluation/results.json.
